game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Leo's Fortune:** Players control Leo, a sentient furball, navigating 24 hand-crafted platforming levels by using a virtual joystick on the left side of the screen for horizontal movement and swiping up on the right side to inflate, allowing Leo to float or roll faster, and swiping down to deflate for precise landings. The core gameplay involves solving physics-based puzzles by manipulating environmental objects, activating switches, and timing movements to avoid various traps and hazards. The objective in each level is to collect all scattered gold coins, which act as breadcrumbs left by a thief, to progress to the next stage. Upon completing all 24 levels, a Hard-core Mode is unlocked, challenging players to finish the entire game without dying, with Game Center leaderboards tracking fastest completion times for each level.
game_info.description: You are Leo, a sentient furball on a quest to recover your stolen gold! Use your unique physical properties to navigate dangerous platforming levels. You can inflate to float across gaps and roll smoothly, or deflate to become heavy, activate switches, and stop precisely. Avoid spikes, collect gold coins to increase your score, and reach the exit portal to progress through the levels.
game_info.controls:
- Arrow Left / Right: Move Leo (Roll)
- Arrow Up: Inflate (Jump / Float / Slide faster)
- Arrow Down: Deflate (Drop fast / Brake / Heavy impact)
- R: Restart Game
- ESC: Pause / Resume
- ENTER: Start Game

TEST_1.strategy_description: The bot attempts to reach the goal by constantly moving right. It jumps (Inflates) if it detects a gap or obstacle ahead, attempting to float over dangers. It aims to traverse all levels to the win screen.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The player character should progress through the levels and eventually reach the "Game Over - Win" state, confirming level logic and physics stability.

TEST_2.strategy_description: The bot alternates between inflating and deflating while moving randomly left and right. This tests the collision resolution and state change logic (size/gravity changes).
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The game should run without crashing or the player getting stuck in geometry. The player should eventually die or stay alive, but the simulation must remain stable.

TEST_3.strategy_description: The bot performs no inputs. This tests the idle animations, particle systems, and environmental movement (if any) without player interference.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: The game runs at 60 FPS, the player settles on the ground, and idle animations (fur movement) continue to play correctly.
